name: New York
description: The Empire State, centered on the city that never sleeps — from Broadway to the Brooklyn Bridge.

questions.q: What is the capital of New York State?

source.notes: State capital since 1797.

questions.q: Which Manhattan borough is home to the Statue of Liberty offshore on Liberty Island?

source.notes: Liberty Island is jurisdictionally part of New York but geographically in Upper New York Bay.

questions.q: How many boroughs make up New York City?

source.notes: Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx, Staten Island.

questions.q: Which iconic 'Big Apple' park, 843 acres of landscaping, stretches down the middle of Manhattan?

source.notes: Designed by Frederick Law Olmsted and Calvert Vaux, opened 1858.

questions.q: Wall Street, home to the New York Stock Exchange, is in which borough?

source.notes: Lower Manhattan's Financial District.

questions.q: Which Manhattan borough is home to Yankee Stadium and the Bronx Zoo?

source.notes: The only borough on the U.S. mainland.

questions.q: Times Square is world-famous for hosting what annual New Year's event?
choices: A fireworks show over the Hudson

source.notes: Held annually since 1907.

questions.q: Before being renamed in 1664, New York City was called what by its Dutch founders?

source.notes: Renamed for the English Duke of York.

questions.q: Which king of England did the city and state get their new name from in 1664?

source.notes: Duke of York at the time of the renaming; later King James II.

questions.q: Which 1883 bridge connects lower Manhattan to Brooklyn?

source.notes: Designed by John A. Roebling.

questions.q: Which iconic building was the tallest in the world when it was completed in 1931?

source.notes: 1,454 ft to its antenna tip.

questions.q: Which major museum stretches along Central Park's east side and is often called 'The Met'?

source.notes: Actually there are multiple 'The Met's' in NYC — the museum is distinct from the Metropolitan Opera.

questions.q: Which NFL team shares MetLife Stadium with the New York Jets?

source.notes: Both teams actually play in New Jersey.

questions.q: Which Manhattan avenue is America's most famous shopping and flagship-store street?

source.notes: Home to Tiffany, Apple's glass cube, Rockefeller Center at 47th-51st.

questions.q: The immigration-processing station that welcomed 12 million newcomers between 1892 and 1954 was called what?

source.notes: Now an immigration museum.

questions.q: Which NHL hockey team plays at Madison Square Garden?

source.notes: The Knicks also play at MSG.

questions.q: The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) is famous for housing which Vincent van Gogh painting?

source.notes: Painted in 1889 from the Saint-Rémy asylum.

questions.q: Which town in upstate New York hosted the Winter Olympics — twice?
Which New York City newspaper is the largest by circulation and has won the most Pulitzers?

source.notes: 'The Gray Lady,' founded 1851.

questions.q: The New York City Marathon runs through all five boroughs, finishing in which park?

source.notes: Starts on Staten Island, finishes at Tavern on the Green.

questions.q: Who was mayor of New York City during the September 11, 2001 attacks?

source.notes: Giuliani served January 1994 - December 2001.

questions.q: The New York Yankees play their home games in which borough?

source.notes: Yankee Stadium in the South Bronx since 1923 (rebuilt 2009).
